options.text: Commence oral di/flucloxacillin 25 mg/kg six-hourly for 7 days, then refer to ENT for elective complete sinus excision once the infection has settled
Refer urgently for total parotidectomy under suspicion of an infected first branchial cleft anomaly with facial nerve and parotid duct involvement
Reassure family that the swelling is self-limiting and arrange follow-up review only if the lesion enlarges, discharges, or develops surrounding cellulitis
Express the sinus contents by squeezing the lesion firmly today, then apply topical mupirocin 2% ointment three times daily for 7 days
Commence oral aciclovir 20 mg/kg five times daily for 7 days as empirical treatment for suspected herpes zoster oticus with otalgia
Refer urgently to paediatric ophthalmology for examination under general anaesthesia and consideration of strabismus surgery despite normal Hirschberg and cover-uncover testing today
Reassure parents this is pseudoesotropia from epicanthic folds, document normal Hirschberg and cover-uncover testing, and advise return for review by the routine 4-year preschool vision screen
Patch the left eye with an adhesive eye patch for 2 hours daily for 3 months to prevent amblyopia despite absent strabismus or amblyopia on objective testing today
Commence topical atropine 1% drops once daily to the right eye to penalise the dominant eye and treat presumed amblyopia in the left eye without specialist diagnosis
Order an outpatient MRI brain and orbits under sedation to exclude an intracranial mass causing a sixth cranial nerve palsy despite full painless eye movements documented today
ECT is absolutely contraindicated because of his prior inferior myocardial infarction
ECT is first-line for severe MDD with psychotic features and food refusal
ECT cannot lawfully proceed because he currently lacks capacity to consent
All antidepressants must be ceased before ECT to avoid serotonin syndrome
Cognitive screening is unnecessary because he has no documented dementia diagnosis
Commence olanzapine 5 mg nocte and arrange routine GP follow-up in two weeks
Refer to the local early-psychosis (headspace / EPPIC-aligned) service for assertive multidisciplinary assessment
Diagnose cannabis-induced psychotic disorder and discharge with brief intervention on cessation
Arrange outpatient MRI brain and EEG before any psychiatric treatment is offered
Admit involuntarily under the relevant state Mental Health Act for assessment
Continue daily thiamine 100 mg orally and observe with hourly CIWA-Ar reassessment overnight
Symptom-triggered diazepam 10-20 mg PO 1-2 hourly titrated to CIWA-Ar plus IV Pabrinex
Commence oral carbamazepine 200 mg three times daily as the sole withdrawal agent
Use an intravenous propofol infusion in a ward-based setting with bedside monitoring
Restart small amounts of oral alcohol to taper consumption over 5 days at home
IV high-dose B-complex (Pabrinex 2 pairs IV TDS), correct magnesium, then continue dextrose
Continue dextrose, give a single 100 mg intramuscular dose of thiamine, then monitor
Hold any further IV glucose and commence intravenous magnesium sulfate as monotherapy
Arrange an urgent MRI brain before commencing any pharmacological replacement therapy
Commence oral thiamine 300 mg daily without any parenteral B-vitamin therapy
Add scheduled risperidone 1 mg BD and continue oxycodone, lorazepam, and metoclopramide
Start donepezil 5 mg daily for worsening Alzheimer disease and discharge home
Transfer to closed psychiatric ward under involuntary order for management of psychosis
Minimise deliriogenic drugs, address constipation, optimise non-pharmacological measures, treat UTI only if supported
Commence intravenous ciprofloxacin empirically for presumed urinary tract infection based on urinalysis
Add lithium augmentation to trough 0.4-0.8 mmol/L with renal and thyroid monitoring
Switch venlafaxine to fluoxetine 20 mg daily as monotherapy without any augmentation
Add bupropion 150 mg daily as monoamine combination augmentation strategy
Add diazepam 5 mg BD as anxiolytic augmentation for six weeks duration
Refer urgently for ECT as first-line treatment for resistant depressive illness
